I should work on the Hoonies - I would like to. I don’t believe it will go anywhere - I want to be realistic - it feels like I’m spinning my wheels. Maybe I could get someone else to keep me accountable - IDK who honestly - I haven’t seen Marlon or Sonia in a while and I could not care less…although I do like Marlon and it would be nice to see them. Told Niha I was camping and plan to be “camping” till Zach gets back on July 3rd.

I have this urge(?) to put the Hoonies into circles together and “summon” something - something abstract. Start with 2. Figure out what’s in common between them. Find something that exemplifies that. OR (?) (/and?) imagine something and let 6-7 things describe it while keeping them in the periphery of my mind while I find them. Maybe Hoonies aren’t the best way to do this - they’re a little bit complicated, and since I make them myself, it’s hard to see if they have too much/too little in common/what. I feel weird that I have no social plans today, but to think about the week stretching on without having to be social feels freeing and I can imagine The Hoonies stretching on into the week.
@@PB@@

When Stanley looked at my food fungus last night he said I was good at asking questions - got to the heart of the matter, and - other people don’t ask questions or don’t understand the answer. It was a nice compliment. i wonder if he was annoyed at all the questions and trying to be positive lol - he showed me stoicism. Then mommy and I were talking about nice compliments that you actually like and remember Olivia saying I make funny side comments that no one catches. Sean said he likes my philosophy and that I’ve thought about things (unlike Jazz). Then I gave mommy some compliments. I should do that more.

Now at Peets - egg + salad + avocado
looking at set theory book - that could remind me all the thoughts that I forgot - “1st Student” - and then I was thinking - remember when I took mushrooms and the roller coasters that I experience on a daily basis became instantaneous, and I could watch them “real time,” instead of just seeing “Oh, that category changed…now I want this…” what if I could create that - write it into a book - simulate the roller coaster “feeling” - with categories that change every second, And (/or?) what if I wrote myself a bible - that no one else could see - that showed me exactly how to do the concepts in stuff - what if I’m dreading showing anything to everyone, which is why I’m putting it off for so long - a little love letter to myself. Reading a bible verse a day (like Stanley) (instead of Stoicism I found my own…)

3:08 PM
At gget - had to put on my dress to make me feel better - took a nap on the couch that was interrupted by one of the stupid playa pals but maybe it’s “like” texting, but without the anxiety of having to respond…saw Sean driving in his window at a light for a while at my table at peets. It was nice to see him and not have to interact with him. Just in time for food and non-awk interaction with the girl that works here. Fell asleep thinking about what peanut butter and cheese have in common and how many worksheets I’ve made recently - get to know you - venn diagram is the easiest conceptually but “emotionally” has to be navigated carefully. Then there is “Star of paradox” - individual for each. Gender proclivities…and I was thinking of having a food worksheet. Also thinking about how the inclusion of the (pic) wooden chest implies a [steadfast] purpose, and the label (pic) says what it is. Anyway - this is the diagram I imagined right before falling asleep. Also: The Difference Between kits and Sets: (pic) they all have something in common, but is it in common with the center, or in common with each other. Also: cognitive vehicles something in common between getting from point A to point B - metaphors and lying to yourself/hiding things from yourself @@PB@@

cognitive vehicles (pic) And the tenets of teaching that I forgot to tell daddy were: metaphors help you get to where you’re going. The other idea: was that: you start with a venn diagram - 2 random hoonies, and what do they have in common. And then you try to find another one that has all these same things in common, and also all the same differences…for ?) simplifying it if you list all the things they have in common 1st, and then 2nd, list all the differences. Anyway, then there is the other thing where you can put the hoonies into different situations (relationships to each other) @@PB@@

Kept taking off my shoes, and then wanting to get water, and not being able to walk across the floor with my feet naked. Then this guy came scootering past my window wearing no shoes, and walked right in, so then I fet comfortable to get water cause he wasn’t kicked out

Thinking about how, when I’m with other people, it’s moe boring because I have to think about whatever they’re thinking about (and it’s usually not that intersting). Different poepl i hang out with have different levels of things i can tell them about what I’m actually thinking about. ex. daddy having potential for high levels of abstraction but he also loves to tell me stuff which stresses me out. Sandy probs too but I don’t want to burden him. Maddie would listen and maybe know what I was saying. Jonathan wouldn’t listen so I have no idea if he understood. Sean would maybe listen but would get mad at the end because some emotional thing that was going on - maybe a competition would get to him…Evan…I wouldn’t tell him because he wouldn’t know what I was talking about @@PB@@

but also maybe he would. Mommy would not know (I don’t think) Also: I was thinking about…baby steps is understanding taht (discrete) things have something in common (one particular thing, and nothing else), advanced id: one thing has things in common with itself - either agrees with itself or doesn’t. - you can pick up on essential characteristics in one particular object - collect evidence that the thing is a certain kind of thing (or collect evidence against it. The Central Paradox - of the gentlemen is about animals and men and being trained and whatever Gus was talking about but the idea behind a Central Paradox is that there are related ideas - slightly different versions of the world that are emitting patterns also. Anyway, that’s what you’re supposed to be thinking about as you watch it. (And if you watch the thing, you get the complete picture, because, like “sage’s faces in places (and spaces)” you get to see the concept in a variety of situations, just like you would see a character act differently circumstantially. @@PB@@

PRIMO PASSO (pic) meat and potatoes peas and carrots START with the plate peas, and carrots, chicken you need to design a plate for these things - to put the carrots, and the chicken, and the peas on. The plate you design includes a large area, and two smaller areas for the sides (peas and carrots) and this makes the right room for the food, with a larger section for the main dish, and the other two sections are smaller. Now that you have this space for the {food}, you can remove the food, and now you can use the same plate, for something else - meatloaf, potatoes, and green beans. Short ribs, waffle fries, brocolli. Always, the plate makes it clear what is the main dish, and what is the sides. Now that it’s clear the “relationships” between the spaces in the plate, @@PB@@

(pic)
(pic)
Now you can replace the interior spaces with things of your choosing
@@PB@@

June 7th When I was showing Sean the metaphor machine it turns out I had an easy version, where it’s basically just one metaphor - a comparison - going in - sweet comes out. And can draw a diagram. Also, with the pattern chapter, I can compare it to the pins board, picking up a cigarette etc. and say what they have in common. (pic)

(pic) and you could put as many as you want, as long as they only refer to one thing… You can use the metaphor machine to “disappear a thought” by comparing it to various things that it is “like,” and it is saved in a certain format - as the examples… (because it is maybe impossible to save an actual concept in itself, because there is nothing concrete to hold onto - the word itself could mean infinite things, but if you save the examples, then you can remind yourself what they each have in common with the AC you’re trying to save and it’s like a drawer pull because you can now open it… @@PB@@
